title: Behavioral Trust Clustering: A Thermodynamic Governance Layer for Production LLMs
description:
A model-agnostic, retraining-free inference-time governance layer that reduces hallucination rate by 52% relative on the official HumanEval benchmark with Qwen2.5-Coder-7B (16.5% to 7.8%, z = 2.12, p < 0.05) at 70% coverage and 92.17% net precision. The method composes (i) self-reported confidence elicited via a calibration-aware system prompt, (ii) semantic equivalence among K stochastically sampled candidates computed by clustering on their executable behavior, and (iii) a closed-form trust score derived from a thermodynamic analogy that admits an exact phase diagram. Targeted at regulated industries (banking, healthcare, legal compliance).

The repository contains the paper PDF, the snc-core Python package (v0.4.0, MIT licensed), the full HumanEval evaluation harness, and the candidate cache for exact replication of the threshold sweeps.
notes: Companion paper for the snc-core Python package (v0.4.0, MIT licensed). All experiments reproducible from the included scripts and candidate cache.
